name: Gaia BH1
companion: Sun-like G star in a 186-day orbit
note: The nearest known black hole — found by the wobble it imposes on its companion star.

name: Gaia BH3
note: The most massive stellar black hole known in the Milky Way — likely born from a nearly metal-free star.

name: Gaia BH2
note: Its wide orbit shows truly dormant black holes exist — no X-rays at all.

name: A0620-00 (V616 Mon)
note: For decades the closest known black hole, revealed by a 1975 X-ray nova outburst.

name: Cygnus X-1
companion: Blue supergiant feeding it via stellar wind
note: The first widely accepted black hole — subject of the famous Hawking–Thorne bet.

name: V404 Cygni
companion: Early K giant in a 6.5-day orbit
note: Erupted spectacularly in 2015; one of the first black holes with a precise parallax distance.

name: Sagittarius A*
companion: Orbited by the S-star cluster at the heart of the Milky Way
note: Our galaxy's central supermassive black hole — imaged by the Event Horizon Telescope in 2022 (Nobel Prize 2020 for proving its existence).
